title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Image and Runtime Lens: Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives
summary: A small accessible web application needs a readable Dockerfile whose build inputs, defaults, process model, and runtime files are explicit. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dockerfile syntax parser directives increment in reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
Plan tag, digest, push, retention, deletion, garbage-collection, and rollback evidence across local and remote stores.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives · Theory Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries. A defensible solution applies Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: dockerfile-syntax-parser-directives — Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-3
  - competency: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-3
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: worked dockerfile build context instructions 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The syntax directive is a comment with no effect on available Dockerfile features. This fails the Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: dockerfile-syntax-parser-directives-pz7447
  - competency: dockerfile-syntax-parser-directives
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: dockerfile syntax parser directives
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: recovery host starting from an empty project namespace
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: cold-build trace and content-store usage comparison
    case-key: pz7447
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
